MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT.

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Marianna Oustinovskaya (408-842-1688; moustinovskaya@wadelitigation.com) of Wade Law Group for Plaintiff.
Jonathan Rolnick (jonathan.rolnick@sfcityatty.org), Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. On February 27, 2019, at a mediation, the parties signed a document entitled "General Settlement Terms." (Reznikov Decl. Para. 3 & Ex. A.) That document was signed by both parties, and contained all of the material terms of their settlement. It is not a mere "agreement to agree," as it stated that it "will be enforceable pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6," and Plaintiff herself filed a notice of settlement of entire case on March 11, 2019. That the agreement was entered into by the City conditional upon subsequent approval by the City Attorney's Office, the Sheriff's Department, and the Board of Supervisors, which was later forthcoming, does not render it unenforceable. Nor does the fact that the parties contemplated later entering into a standard form settlement agreement. (See Elyaoudahyan v. Hoffman (2003) 104 Cal.App.4th 1421, 1429 [rejecting contention that settlement was not enforceable because it "contemplated a subsequent written agreement" that plaintiff did not sign].) Consistent with section 664.6, the Court will enter judgment pursuant to the terms in the General Settlement Terms document.

order and judgment signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D.
9:33 am. The matter is passed so an In-camera hearing can be held.
9:50 am. Matter is recalled. An In-Camera hearing is held with Ms. Green and Atty. Randle.
10:05 am, on the record. The Court speaks with Defendant's Counsel and informs him of the Court's suggestion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

LAW OFFICE OF MURLENE RANDALL, ATTY. MURLENE RANDALL'S MOTION TO BE RELEIVED AS COUNSEL (MANCONIA GREEN)

Granted as of today. The proceedings are stayed until 2/15/20 or such earlier time as Plaintiff files a substitution of Counsel. The Court also requests the Order to Show Cause hearing set for 1/28/20 be continued for 2 months.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Two orders are signed. (see scanned order for details)
